title: scorzonera mollis subsp. idaea
intro: Scorzonera mollis subsp. idaea, also known as Idaea scorzonera, is a type of perennial plant in the sunflower family that is native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Scorzonera mollis subsp. idaea include its potential to relieve symptoms of arthritis, gout, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its ability to support kidney function and promote the elimination of toxins from the body. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it can be consumed raw or cooked as a vegetable, although its use as a food is less common due to its bitter taste.
